You're a weird one, you know, a lone swordsman, coming over every year from... I forgot, are you from Jiangqi or Yumen?
I don't stay in one place. The four corners are my home.
But I remember that you come here to this tea house in this little town every autumn to meet someone... how many years has it been?
Seven.
Seven years... all you do when you meet is have a cup of tea, have a little chat. You're neither friends nor lovers; you don't even know her name, do you?
We promised not to pry into each other's matters.
Strange things indeed...
Who exactly is this person you're waiting for, anyway?
I don't know.
I've dealt with many people in my time... good, bad, wise, foolish, they're not so hard to understand at the end of the day.
But she's the only one whom I've never figured out.
Your sword just knocked over my tea, young lady.
It's rather cramped in here. You should put away that sword of yours.
It's just a cup of tea. I'll pay for it.
In fact, I'll buy you a jug of wine, and call you my friend.
And you can teach your new friend how to use a sword.
Me? Haha...
You're talking to the wrong guy. I'm no swordsman, just an ordinary merchant delivering some goods to the City of Dusk, stopping by this town for a cup of tea.
I don't even have a sword on me. What kind of swordsman doesn't carry a sword?
Don't play the fool with me. I won't let you go until you say yes.
Wh-Why would you cut the table in half like that? You're scaring the other guests...
Shut up.
I'll pay for it later. Don't interrupt my chat with my friend.
Say any more, and I'll cut you in half too.
A-Alright...
...That's not very reasonable, is it?
What point is there in being reasonable? Better to roam free in the jianghu.
But one needs plenty of skill to roam free.
I know you're a master of the sword. I want to learn from you.
*sigh*...
You came a little too late.
I lost my sword...
...The moment it disappeared, I vowed to retire from the jianghu.
You lost your sword?
I lost it.
When?
Last night.
I stopped by this time for the night. It was gone when I woke up in the morning.
It just disappeared without a trace. No thief ever came, nor could they have taken anything from me if they did.
What kind of sword was it?
A good one.
One who was by my side for many years. Without it, my business would not have prospered so much, nor would I have had the fame that I enjoy in the jianghu.
Why don't you look for it, if it's so important?
On the contrary, I can't look for it.
Why?
For so many years, I've used the sword, washed it, used it again. Nightmares and waking up from them... it seemed like there was nothing else in my life.
The sword does not remember the blood that it has tasted, but I can never shake the sin... perhaps losing my sword was a sign from Heaven.

I will rid myself of the sword and the status it brought me. Perhaps I'll change my face and name, and find a quiet place to live out the rest of my days... I'll leave the jianghu and bid farewell to my life before.
That's why I can't teach you.

The tidings of fate have brought us together in this tiny teahouse. If you don't mind me being a busybody, I have a word of advice for you... don't learn the ways of the sword, and cut all ties with the jianghu.
You can't rid yourself of it.
Why?
You're lying.
You're tired of the killing, of the constant cycle of revenge, but you could never let go of the freedom, of the days when you loved and hated as you pleased.
You just wanted an excuse to quit. Losing your sword gave you a convenient one.
You're talking like you know me...
Shall we make a bet? We'll see each other again in this very same tea house, same time next year.
Go and live a year without your sword, as you intend.
If you feel happier, I won't bother you again, nor ask you to teach me your swordsmanship.
I can learn it, whether you teach me or not.
Hah... sure. It's a bet.
I may or may not teach you the sword, but I'll buy you a drink the next time we meet.

lines.st: The following year...

lines.x: You're here already... why don't you go in?
No hurry. There's plenty of space.
Huh?
I've been studying the sword every day of the past year, and I've figured out a few moves. I'll show you.
You don't need to offer any advice, just watch.
Heh... sure.
Go ahead.
And that's it... duoxie.
Why do you thank me?
Without a master like you, I wouldn't know which moves should be polished, and which moves should be scrapped.
I didn't need you to say anything. All I needed was your reaction.
Your face told me all I needed to know about which moves stand out, and which moves are flawed.
You said you didn't want to take up the sword again, but you looked like an inveterate drunkard who just saw a jug of wine. You can't hide it.
The jianghu is too dangerous for a simple one like you.
Haha, you're absolutely right.
Interesting... ouch—
Here, take this medicine.
I thought you would keep holding your pain.
The wound opened when I laughed.
What happened?
I was waylaid on my way out of Jiangqi, after delivering the last batch of goods for the season.
Enemies of yours?
Yeah.
A job that I took a few years ago... it was only after I killed the man that I realized he wasn't just an evil rich man. Many people had reason to thank him.
And reason to take revenge for him, even though they were not his kin.
They must have watched me for a long time. They finally dared to attack when they were sure that I had lost my sword, and I hadn't fought in a long time.
I barely escaped with my life to meet you here.
How many enemies came for your life in this past year?
Too many to remember.
And you just kept running?
What was I supposed to do, let them kill me?
You could have killed those petty minions easily, sword or no sword.
To what end? One can never truly kill all of one's enemies. Kill one, and you make another.
I'll just keep sinking deeper.
And what of it?
Isn't that what the jianghu is about? Live free and live without regret. If your sword-hand slows down and you fall to someone stronger, that's just how things are.
You've seen the best and worst of the jianghu. What's so bad about such a life?
Besides, is laying low and keeping your head down the rest of your life really the way you want to live?
I still think losing my sword was a sign from Heaven.
(Frowns without comment)
Come in, friends.
Your tea is ready. You should drink it before it gets cold.
Let's go. You can leave once you've had your tea.
But I just arrived...
My swordsmanship has improved greatly in the past year, but you haven't figured out how to live without the sword. I won the bet this year.
See you again next year.
...Very well.

lines.st: Year three...

lines.x: You're still alive.
How have you been? I don't see many new wounds. Have your enemies grown fewer, or have you learned to run faster?

lines.st: Year four...

lines.x: 'Marking a boat to find a sword'? How could anyone be so stupid?
Although I've never seen a boat, nor the river, nor the sea.
'Lotus leaves as far as the eye could see, an endless green becoming one with the sky...' I wonder what it looks like.

lines.st: Year five...

lines.x: See you next year.

lines.st: Year six...

lines.x: Hey!
Oh, there you are.
I got within two inches of you, and you didn't notice... I could have killed you easily, if I wanted to.
You still can.
Hmph. You were distracted by something else... what are you hiding?
Oh—
Notice, warrant, bounty...
'Delivering Blade'... is it the name of you and your sword?
Yeah.
You keep talking about ridding yourself of your sword, getting away from the jianghu, then you commit all these crimes?
Not me. The Delivering Blade.
What do you mean?
The Delivering Blade, which had been quiet for so many years, has once again become active in recent months, leaving a trail in multiple nomadic cities, Jiangqi, Langyi, Shangshu, Lungmen...
Or should I say, a trail of blood.
I told you last year that medicinal herb harvests were poor across the board. I had a really tough year, trying to my debts settled with a few major clients...
You mean someone's stealing your name to do evil? Attempting to frame you directly, even?
Why?
For twenty years I've worked for both the law and the underworld. There must be countless warrants and bounties on my head, but there's one thing I can be sure—
Whether they're trying to catch people's attention, or hide their true aim... the name of the Delivering Blade is convenient for them.
Which means the Delivering Blade can never escape the jianghu.
Funny, isn't it?
You're concerned?
It's been six years since I lost my sword. This was a sign from Heaven for me to distance myself from all this... and I almost succeeded.
When I first set foot into the jianghu, I just wanted to make a living by the sword while doing a few good deeds along the way. But the further I went, the less my will was my own.
It's only now that I realize the jianghu is a vortex, a gigantic vortex, and there's only one way out of it—
Don't get sucked in, in the first place.
You're in pain.
I've never seen you like this before.
Is that so? Perhaps.
...Enough about me. What about you?
Me?
...It's true. You could have killed me if you wanted to, just now.
Your skills have improved tremendously over the last few years.
No secret tomes, no teacher, not even a sparring partner in this little town. You've come this far on your own talent alone...
You don't even feel surprised or confused about all that's happened to me. Your mental attainment, too, is far beyond the ordinary.
The first time we met, I tried to dissuade you from studying the sword, out of my own experiences alone. But perhaps...
Perhaps you're a true genius who belonged in the jianghu all along.
You should leave this town and see what lies outside.
You're right.
It's something that I must do.
Young lady...
What is it?
Frankly, there aren't many who would be a match for you at this point. There's no need to linger in this tiny town. If you want to leave, you should.
We met each other by the tidings of fate. I would like to give you a present, if you'd take it—the name of the Delivering Blade.
Call yourself his student, even the Delivering Blade himself. I could even give you a token to prove it... the jianghu can be a treacherous place, after all, and it'll make life a lot easier for you.
You should go. Let's bring this annual meeting to an end.
It's not time yet.
What?
I haven't found a way to leave this town... but I will, soon.
I don't understand...
You're not the only one who's being drawn into the vortex... see you next year.
Another four hours have passed. It's long past the appointed time, isn't it?
She's never missed the time before.
You're not going?
L-Let me stay a little longer.
*sigh*...
I suggest you don't wait anymore.
I knew there was something you wanted to say, keeper. Go ahead, say it.
The woman you're waiting for is a killer who's committed multiple murders in these parts.
The whole town's in an uproar. Nobody dares to go out, even in the light of day. Didn't you notice how few patrons were in here?
Finally, a Tianshi from the bureaus passed by here a little while back, she's now going after this woman.
So... I don't think she's coming.
She might be aloof and eccentric, but she's not evil...
What happened?
I heard she was looking for a secret tome meant to improve one's kung fu, or something else. I don't know the specifics.
You know... I've been wondering about something.
Go on.
Her sword is unusual.
As far as I know, there were no other martial artists in this town before.
How could a girl who was born and raised here, a town isolated from everything, become so obsessed with the sword and jianghu?
...Born and raised here?
No, she's not local. She just showed up one day, and no one knows what her name is, or where she came from.
Do you know when that was?
Let me think... about the same time that you first came.
...I had just lost my sword then.
Wh-What's going on?
Lin Qingyan, is it? Why do you keep coming for me?
Object of evil, submit and answer for your crimes.
What do you think you know?!
I do have many questions. Why did the Grandmaster's Book of Kung Fu show up here, and why are you after it?
But you won't answer them unless I take you in.
Enough talk. Do your worst—
You missed our appointment.
I thought you had left.
...Zheng Chou, the Delivering Blade?
I don't remember ever doing business with the Tianshi Bureaus.
Lin Qingyan of the Tianshi Bureaus. I read your files when I was working at the Judicial Review Office. It was a thick one. You have been quiet the last few years, though. I see you were hiding here.
And so I end up finding a Judicial Official who knows about me in this place. What a joke, isn't it, trying to retire from the jianghu?
Are you in league with each other?
Didn't you hear him? He left the jianghu behind, trying to wash its stains from his hands these seven years.
He has nothing to do with this secret tome or the entire incident.
But he's trying to protect you.
Protect me? I don't need it.
Hm...
Young lady...
Get out of the way. I can deal with her myself.
The swordsman does not move.
You finally got rid of your sword, of the identity that it gave you. You spent seven years putting your previous life to rest. There's no need to break your vow now...
We barely know each other.
When we first met, you said... I could never rid myself of it.
In fact, it's always been there by my side.
What?
I lost my sword here. Then I met you, who seemed to be born to wield a sword, improving simply by observing my tiniest of reactions...
Of course you could. We were inseparable, once!
I don't know what happened, or why you're doing all this...
Hah. Now you realize. How slow of you.
Funny, isn't it, how you tried to give me the name 'Delivering Blade' when we met last year... when in fact that is exactly what I am.
This is a strange town. There's a mysterious power in the air, causing inanimate objects to become sentient geists.
Teapots, fans, rocks... maybe half the people you see on the streets were once such trinkets.
I don't remember how we got separated, but I've looked like this from my first ever waking moment.
I looked for you, until I found you at the teahouse. You were drinking tea nonchalantly, not the slightest bit anxious. I came up to you and drew your attention, and you said...
I lost my sword. It's a sign from Heaven.
Young lady, no, Delivering Blade, no, how should I call you...?
Don't get sentimental.
A swordsman with blood on his hands wants to turn over a new leaf. That's not wrong. The grumpy Tianshi over there might even exclaim, 'There's no greater virtue.'
But there was one thing that I realized at the time—
This jianghu, which you've grown so tired of... I've seen it and walked it with you, but I've never experienced it myself.
I shall wield the sword and travel the jianghu—this time, under my own name!
But Waregeists cannot leave this town.
I've tried many times. The river to the east, the bridge to the west... it's as though there's an invisible wall around the town, one that stops only the geists!
That's why you said 'It's not time yet'...
...I knew it.
You stole the Book of Kung Fu because you sought to break this barrier for geists through the Grandmaster's teachings.
Such strange affairs. You could not have known who the Grandmaster was, so why would you think his writings would have such power... someone is trying to manipulate geists into causing trouble.
Are you done with your deduction, Tianshi?
Yes.
I thought you might have been a geist, but I didn't expect you to have such a past with Zheng Chou, the Delivering Blade... yet it provides no reference for my judgment of your crimes.
You must answer for the acts you committed in attempting to acquire the book.
Submit, or be annihilated in form and spirit by Lei Fa.
Submit?
The secret tome is already mine. Soon I'll discover its secret and leave this place... this place that I've been trying to get out of for seven years!
No more swordbearer, no more sheath. What is there to stop the sword's edge?
The only way to stop it is to break it!
If that's so...
Thunder of heaven, heed my call.
Lin Qingyan's voice is soft, but it seems as though heaven and earth themselves heed her call. The sky suddenly darkens, dark clouds gather, and a bright arc of electricity forms on her palm.
Tianshi Lin, I don't know how things came to this, but... I can understand why she wanted to do what she did. There's no need to...
She's my sword. I-I'll accept responsibility with her...
Begone!
Fool!
I can understand her desire for freedom.

But violence is inherent in her nature as the Geist of a sword. It is likely that she cannot control herself anymore; the more intent her desire is, the more important it is that I stop her from putting even more innocent people in harm's way.
As for you...
It was a good thing that you took the loss of your sword as a sign to distance yourself from the jianghu and its conflicts. But seven years have passed, and you have yet to fully retire from it.
Hatred and grudges are like snow falling in a valley, piling up deeper and deeper, but you could have freed yourself from it if you truly tried.
Maybe she was right... in your heart, did you really throw away your sword?
Enough talk. As both the Deputy Minister of the Judicial Review Office, and disciple of the Tianshi Bureaus—this thunder must strike!
The clouds and thunder scatter. The sword-bearing woman is gone, and the skies are clear again.
A plain-bound book lies where she stood. The wind blows its cover open—
'Book of Kung Fu'.
(The one who stole the Book of Kung Fu from Yumen might be here in this town. What is this person planning?)
(No use thinking about it. I'll just retrieve the Book of Kung Fu for now...)
There you are!
I've been looking for you!
...Huai Tianpei?
There is only the swordsman left on the street.
He stands there in a daze, staring silently into space.
Such commotion... is something the matter?
I lost my sword.
It's time to go...
Once a year... and never again.
Excuse me, sir...
Y-You're...
Do you know me?
I'm lost. I don't know how I got here. You're the first person to pass by in a while, and I wonder if you know the way.
(So that's what Tianshi Lin meant...)
In your heart, did you really throw away your sword?
I don't know the answer.
But what you're doing is heartless, Tianshi Lin.
Heartless, is it?
Are you still going to beg for mercy for her, even at this hour?
We saw each other one day of the year for seven years. I thought I was the one guiding her, but it turns out it was the other way around.
It's true, I was a fool. I had thrown away my sword, only to pick it up time and again.
I don't know what I'm clinging to, or what I'm afraid of... maybe it's something I should figure out, in a remote village somewhere, or in a prison cell.
I'll pay for all the damage she caused, then I'll turn myself in. Before that, though, please...
(Frenzied muttering)
Look at her. She can't hear you anymore.
She has engaged in too much violence to obtain the book. Malice will overcome her mind, even if the thunder of Heaven does not strike.
But she will never truly be human, even if Lei Fa manages to rid her of geisthood.
The living suffer much and think much. But the geists formed from inanimate objects are often simple of mind... it is difficult for them to truly become part of society.
Is it an act of kindness to bring them into this world, or an act of cruelty?
(Perhaps you should beg the Tianshi Bureaus' forgiveness when all is said and done, Lin Qingyan...)
But...
Enough talk. As both the Deputy Minister of the Judicial Review Office, and disciple of the Tianshi Bureaus—this thunder must strike!
What did you just say?
Don't you remember anything?
Remember what?
N-Never mind.
There's a town down this path. The Chidao's half a day's journey from there, which leads to many nomadic cities, Shangshu, Dahuang, Langyi...
Where are you going?
Where do you want to go?
Forward.
I'm not in great health, but I want to see those places...
Perhaps we can travel together, for a while.
What is this...?
That madman barged into the training grounds and just started brawling. I think he's the one who fought the Grandmaster in this same place six months ago.
We don't know what's wrong with him, but we tried to avoid hurting him. He knocked a few dozen guys out cold, and then just went away.
...Huai Tianpei?
What madness is this? What happened to the Grandmaster's sword...?
General!
Someone just broke into the armory!
What did we lose?
The Grandmaster's Book of Kung Fu.

lines.st: Dear General Zuo,
War is on the horizon. The lives of your soldiers and the livelihoods of your people must be your priority.
The best strategy is to disrupt your enemy's strategy. Non-aggression is the essence of justice.
I have borrowed your book for a time. It will be returned to you in due course.
Mo Yi, Shanhaizhong
Best Regards

lines.x: The contents of the armory are unmolested, except for an empty space on the bookshelf.
There is only a letter where a book should be.
Guards!
Yes, sir!
What are your orders?
Prepare a Messenger. I have a letter to be delivered to the Sui Regulator.
Yes, sir.
And... a second Messenger, to deliver a secret message to the Tianshi Bureau.
Understood.

Impressive, Master Huai. Stealing an army camp ten thousand strong and escaping without breaking a sweat... no one in Yan's history could have achieved such a feat, and it's unlikely anyone else ever will.
Huai Tianpei does not answer, striding around in circles, muttering under his breath.
No need to worry, Master Huai. You helped me obtain the book, and I'll help you in turn.
You will have a bout with the Grandmaster, in an arena with all eyes upon you.
I will prepare everything for you ten days hence, at the peak of Mt. Mang.
...Uh-huh.
But I want ask you, Master Huai... no mortal man is your equal, and you know that the Grandmaster is no mortal man. Why do you insist on defeating him?
Humans have not held their own against Beasts for thousands of years by pitting their own weakness against their foes' strength.
You're not making any sense.
If someone's stronger than me, I train until I beat them. If heaven and earth are stronger than me, I'll do whatever it takes to beat them.
This obsession of yours is not fit for a mortal man.
But you would not have been forced into your current circumstances by the 'merchant', if you'd completely forgotten your humanity.
Should I pity you, or laugh at you?
Interesting...
Dad came this way, Uncle Lee?
The footprints are widely-spaced, but the distance between them is consistent, and every print is equal in depth.
They could only have been left by a kung fu master.
This looks like a mangler's claws...
You're making fun of me again.
Hehe...
I caught one glimpse of him that time, and then he got away... this is the only way now, based on the clues that we have.
We deal with smart people in smart ways. But your dad is a kung fu-obsessed fool... so we handle him the foolish way.
Just keep looking...
Are you worried about him, or angry that he's making trouble again?
I just... don't understand.
No one would ever call him reliable, but he's always honest and upright when it comes to kung fu.
Never bully the weak just because you're strong, and never hurt the innocent. He raised me on these ideals.
I don't get it. What changed him?
I've heard that people lose their reason when they lose what they love, or if they let their obsessions get the better of them...
Is there anyone under heaven who could break his heart over kung fu?
Unless—
Unless he's gone so far down the martial path that he's no longer dealing in mortal affairs...
Well... I haven't forgiven him yet, but I won't forgive whoever turned him this way either!
You're right... they won't get away with it.
But you just reminded me...
There's a town near here. Perhaps it'll offer a clue.
You're looking for someone?
This has been a busy clinic lately. You see those people with plaster casts all over them? Maybe the one you're looking for is among them.
Looks like business has been booming for you.
Mind if I ask... why are there so many wounded here?
It's a long story... this has always been a peaceful town, with no real martial tradition.
Maybe a couple of people make a living with the sword, but nobody worth writing home about. Best they can do is to stand guard at a manor, or escort some goods a short ways.
In recent days, though, we've been getting jianghu adventurers from all across the country coming to these parts, for some reason.
More people means more trouble. These guys are showing up with hot tempers. A little argument, and fists start flying.
They're in pretty bad shape. What kind of argument gets them going at each other like that?
That's another matter altogether...

The folks who've come to this town are after some kind of secret kung fu tome. Legend has it, the thing will make you First Under Heaven. And there's nothing they wouldn't do to get their hands on it.
First Under Heaven... again.
There's one particular nutcase among their ranks.
He challenges anyone who looks like a jianghu adventurer, whether they want to fight or not.
Total nutcase, but incredibly strong. Half the people who've been in here these past few days got hurt fighting him.
...Is this guy still in town?
Who knows? He's made his share of enemies. People might be after him now.
Do you know that madman?
Well...
Don't worry, we came to put a stop to the conflict...
Either way, you'd better get him out of here, if you can.
What happens in the jianghu may stay in the jianghu, but the Tianshis and imperial court aren't going to keep quiet if this carries on.
Anyway, I've got patients to see. I hope I have enough medicine in stock...
That sounds like Dad alright.
More likely than not.
But I've never heard of this secret tome they're fighting over.
I've never heard of such a powerful tome either.
Whether it's true or not, there's one thing for sure: A treasure's whereabouts only become publicly known when it's being used for bait.
And we should try to hook whoever set this bait.
Hm?
I think I just saw a familiar face...
You are... Mr. Lee? We met in Shangshu, correct?
Are you looking for the martial master drawn here by a supposed secret kung fu tome?
We don't know if he was drawn here by the tome.
What brings you here, Master Lin?
I'm looking for the one who brought the tome here.
What do you know of all this?
Not much.
All I know is that it is an elaborate plot, and many have come to this town believing those claims.
As for the true purpose behind it all...
They must be trying to put on a show, the way they've drawn so many... what's the best place for a show in a town like this?
Do I hear some commotion outside?
I think this is the second time we've met, Mr. Yun.
I suppose it is fate that determined we should meet again. What a pleasure.
Young lady...
You can call me Mo Yi.
Why have you called for me, Miss Mo?
You are the Grandmaster's favorite student. No one knows him better. We would like you to appraise a certain artifact.
Tell me, is this book a product of his pen?
Intriguing...
All the troubles that have befallen this town over the tome were part of your plan, weren't they?
Let's just say... ask no questions and you'll be told no lies.
In any case, I must ask you to appraise the authenticity of this Book of Kung Fu for Mr. Liang here.
Mo Yi takes out the tome and hands it to Yun Qingping.
This is the second time that Yun Qingping has seen the book in this town. He saw the waiter steal the book in the teahouse, but now it has ended up in Mo Yi's hands.
(Flips through the pages)
Yes, this is the book that the Grandmaster dictated to me, which I put to paper with my pen.
This was a gift from the Grandmaster to General Zuo Xuanliao of Yumen. How did it end up in your hands?
We can talk about that later—is the master of Garden of Grotesqueries interested in this, Mr. Liang?
It is a remarkable feat, obtaining this book. I won't ask how you did it.
I shall communicate this to my master, for him to make a decision.
Will your master still not show himself, even though we've confirmed that this is the original manuscript?
It is not my place to judge on behalf of my master. He has his own standards for determining whether or not an item is truly treasure.
I will give you the answer once I've spoken to him.
What a shame...
Can I ask the question now, Miss Mo?
Why have you stolen the tome and spread such rumors through the jianghu?
You have one foot in the jianghu, don't you?
I accompanied my master on his travels in my early years, but I would not count myself as a kung fu master.
Neither would I. I'm just a jianghu nobody with no knowledge of kung fu.
But I love watching self-proclaimed kung fu masters kill each other like starving beasts for a tiny bit of fame, some ridiculously insignificant squabble.
The quarrels and bickering of real people is so much more interesting than staged plays.
Much blood will be spilt for this play of yours.
Isn't it a little... dishonorable?
What do heaven and earth know of honor? Why should I care?
Just sit back and relax. I'm sure you'll enjoy the show too.
All these things... what's going on?
Am I imagining things? Did all those chairs and tables on the street turn into people? Even the brooms?
No, you're not...
There's something strange about this town. Just think of the objects turning into people as some kind of illusion, and don't let it bother you too much.
Well, I've seen stranger things...
These abominations... could there be one of Miss Ling's siblings in this place?
...I'm afraid the situation is slightly worse than that.
But I've never seen them in an uproar like this. What happened?
Looks like they're heading in the same direction.
Are they... going up the hill?
The peak of Mt. Mang is packed with people, not just jianghu wanderers chasing rumors, but also the non-human residents of the town who have taken human form.
It's an honor to have so many heroes of the jianghu assembled here today.
I'm sure your time is valuable, so I'll get straight to the point. Here it is—!
Mo Yi raises the Book of Kung Fu up high, and many in the crowd begin to whisper to one another.
This is the legendary tome written by the Grandmaster defending the borders of Yan.

Not only does it contain the greatest secrets of kung fu to elevate your school above all others, it also reveals the secret to shedding your mortal hide and becoming a god in flesh, reaching the heights of the Grandmaster!
All who practice kung fu dream of elevating their schools above all others. For many in the crowd, however, they are more interested in the 'shedding your mortal hide and becoming a god' part.
Whatever they are seeking, Mo Yi's words are enough to send them into a frenzy.
With all the rumors flying around the jianghu lately, I'm sure one book would not be enough to convince you.
This is why I've invited Mr. Yun here.
Mr. Yun is the Grandmaster's record keeper. Perhaps some of you even recognize him, and know that I speak the truth.
He has attested to the authenticity of the book. There can be no greater assurance.
Mo Yi pauses to ensure that the crowd does not doubt her before continuing.
I'm young and unlearned, and it is not my place to keep this treasure for my own. That's why I'm hosting this 'Banquet of Heroes'.
All who have assembled here today—this tome shall belong to whoever manages to take it and keep it!
It is as though the crowd has exploded.
Everyone has weapons in hand, brandishing at the people around them. All they need is a cue to start killing.
Those who know better try back off quietly, but even more rush towards Mo Yi, drawn by her promise.
The geists who share the same thought join flesh-and-blood humans in the scrum.
Everyone! Hear me out! Please!
In the commotion, Yun Qingping walks to the front of the stage, and speaks to the crowd below.
Some of them hear his words, and one after another they stop doing whatever they were doing, curious to hear what this frail young man has to say.

My master says that testing oneself against another is the only path to self-reflection, and competing against others is the only path to self-improvement. Competition is a virtue for students of the martial arts.
But we must not forget the most basic rule of kung fu: hard work is the only path to mastery.

The study of the martial arts is, fundamentally, the improvement of oneself. To take shortcuts to make oneself stronger for the sake of fame and glory is to confound the end with the means to that end.
...What are you trying to say?
There is no doubt that this is the original manuscript of the Book of Kung Fu. However, what you should know is—
This book was dictated to me by my master, and put to paper by my pen. It is not imbued with any of the magical effects that Miss Mo claims.
If this book is, in fact, so wondrous, why is it that I, who wrote the words with my own hand, remain an ordinary man, with no kung fu to boast of?
His voice is not loud, but every word rings clear. A commotion of a different kind begins to spread through the crowd.
Mr. Yun, I suggest you refrain from making any wrong moves at a time like this...
Yun Qingping pretends not to hear the girl's threat and continues speaking.

The Book of Kung Fu contains the Grandmaster's understanding of kung fu accumulated over his vast lifetime, as well as key points often missed by martial artists during training. It is of great benefit to any practitioner of the martial arts.
Other than that, it has no unique properties.
If you don't believe me...
Yun Qingping takes out a printed copy of the Book of Kung Fu.
This Book of Kung Fu is no secret, and certainly not one-of-a-kind. There is no need to spill blood over it...
Watch out, Uncle Lee!
Ow...
You know, this is a sight that I didn't really need to see again...
There're too many of them...
Do these pots and pans turned humans have something to do with that 'secret tome' too?
These pots and pans must be headed in the direction of the culprit. We take them in custody, and everything else will become clear.
I would like to ask the two of you to help.
Of course, Master Lin! I'll clear a path for you.
What did you say...?

It was my master's most sincere wish to help more people go further down the path of kung fu. But teaching martial arts is a very personal thing, and the Grandmaster's time and energy are not unlimited.
What he could do was record his thinking and understanding in concise language, so that those who come after can avoid certain pitfalls without learning the hard way.
The Book of Kung Fu is certainly his labor of love, with countless generations of record keepers' archives that I had the honor of organizing.
The Grandmaster gifted this book to General Zuo in the hopes that its teachings would be known throughout the army, and eventually by all the people. He never intended to keep it secret.
In other words, if those present want to take their kung fu to a higher level, I am happy to request another print run. It would be trivial to get everyone a copy.
Profanities begin flying out from the crowd, after a brief moment of silence.
If one person took a step forward, the rest would follow, to throw this Liberi fraud from the mountaintop.
Good... very good...
You've learned courage from the Grandmaster, if nothing else.
But did it occur to you that I could take your life here and now with ease, if you so pushed me?
Then I would be an unlucky man. It can't be helped.
I'm no martial artist, but I know the rules of the jianghu. I can't stand idly by when injustice occurs.
Please, return the Book of Kung Fu to Yumen. It's nothing special to these jianghu adventurers.
But it means a lot to General Zuo.
You... how dare you...?
Allow me to say a few more words, Miss Mo.
You said you wanted to put on a show, of real-life people quarreling and bickering.
I suppose you wanted me to see people spilling blood for the sake of a kung fu tome. All I see, however, is people who want to improve their martial skills.
There's nothing wrong with pursuing mastery of the martial arts.
I've seen much evil in the world, but very little done for the sake of evil. More often, so-called evil begins with good intentions, only incorrect means.
I don't know why you're doing this, but deceiving people like this is not the proper way.
Your skills and your cleverness could be used to better ends.
You may not have learned kung fu from the Grandmaster, but it looks like you did learn this holier-than-thou attitude.
Don't blame me then—
I have you at last...
You are guilty of theft from a critical military facility, and now you are deceiving the people with your lies! What is your purpose?
Ah, this must be the famous Deputy Minister Lin.
But you've already resigned your position, haven't you? It's not your style to overstep your bounds, is it?
As a Tianshi, I cannot stand by and watch this evil take place.
I'm taking you to Baizao.
Haha...
Hahaha...
What are you laughing at?
I'm laughing at how short-sighted you are, despite all the big words you say.
All of Baizao and Yumen are on the brink, yet you're trying to sabotage me while claiming to represent justice.
What do you mean?
Perhaps I don't understand kung fu as deeply as you do...
But you don't understand the Feranmuts nearly as deeply as I!
I didn't want to do this, but...
Mo Yi leaps back and creates some distance.
Standing at the summit, she holds the tome in her hand, with rare focus and reverence in her eyes.
The nine provinces are dark—
The sky darkens at her words.
Heaven and earth are silent—
Heed my words, great mystery—
In the earth, lines warp and weft; in the heavens, the genesis of martial prowess—
In the name of the Shanhaizhong, I bid ye cometh, First of the Twelve, the name of which is Shuo!
The darkness that engulfed heaven and earth is suddenly dashed apart, revealing the manifestation of the bestial Sui, whose towering figure reaching towards the heavens.
All present are starkly reminded of their insignificance, even standing at the peak of a mountain.
Whether this is really Him or simply an illusion, the appearance of this massive shadow signifies one thing: the power of Shuo lives within the manuscript.
(Rumbling roar)
What...?!
...Master?
I suppose there are few living people in Yan who have seen this aspect of Him.
It's a reminder of what Yan has been facing all these years.
If you want to catch me, you'll have to fight Him first.
Look out!
The massive shadow slams its palm towards the crowd on the ground. The entire mountain shakes.
Many are knocked off their feet. But it is no simple blow; Yun Qingping could see clear form and meaning in the palm strike.
With a swiftness that belies its size, its every movement and its presence announce its nature as the manifestation of kung fu itself.
Scatter! Get out of the way!
I call upon the Five Thunders—
Thunder hits the shadow, but it is as though it had struck the void.
It's no use?!
What under heaven is that thing...?
No... that's not the Grandmaster.
The Grandmaster's sword is not here. That is no more than an illusion reflected in the Book of Kung Fu.
I don't know how the girl did it, but there must be a way to break this spell...
If we can bypass the illusion and attack her directly—
Finally...
We meet again...
As the crowd scatters, this 'Shuo' greets its first challenger.
Stay back! It's dangerous!
Master Huai?!
Huai Tianpei does not answer, striding toward 'Shuo' with firm steps.
I lost the sword. I broke my promise. I should not have come to see you.
My dream has yet to be realized. My life is finite. I must come to see you.
(Menacing murmur)
The fist moves winds and clouds.
Huai Tianpei does not dodge or cower, letting the wake of the punch brush against him.
Another punch, heavy as a mountain, inexorable as the tide, falls upon Huai Tianpei.
Fist meets fist. Strength meets strength.
H-How is this possible?
It is the shadow of a god, the manifestation of kung fu itself, yet somehow it has met its match.
It is a duel between equals.
Yun Qingping takes out pen and paper by instinct.
The palm strikes, shattering heaven and earth.
Inhale, exhale, and let the breath be one with the cosmos.
Fist meets palm, still evenly matched.
Huai Tianpei matches 'Shuo' move for move, continuing to step forward. With each exchange, 'Shuo' becomes more corporeal, more real.
One round after another. Finally, Huai Tianpei stands at the summit, gazing up at the illusion's wrath.
...Eleventh rounds?
Yun Qingping flips open his Record of Martial Combat, to the page where he recorded that duel for the ages. He still remembers the final punch, which could not be described with language.
Master Huai has taken another step forward...
Simplicity is perfection. It is a simple thing to understand.

But with life and death on the line, how many could set aside their ego, set aside themselves, cutting everything down to its most simple elements: a single punch or kick at exactly the right time, be it in offense or in defense.
Kung fu has become an inherent part of him, in his limbs, in his muscles, in his viscera.
Thirty rounds have passed. Huai Tianpei still stands.
The power of the punches have left two deep ditches in the boulder beneath his feet.
But he can go no further forward. One more step, and he will fall off the cliff.
Huai Tianpei shows no intention of backing down.
No... he's in danger!
But who dares intervene in such a duel? Who can?
Dad!
The cry causes the man to pause for an instant, in the midst of a fight for life and death.
It's you...
Well...
...I remember now.

lines.st: I remember now.
I made a forty-year promise.
I handed over the sword, because I could not abandon it...
I remember now.
I have a daughter to protect...
My name is... Huai Tianpei.
But I forgot.
I forgot all the moves I learned.
I forgot my opponents and enemies.
I have never lost. I am without equal under heaven.
Who am I fighting against?
I am only fighting against myself.
There is no path ahead. I must turn back.

lines.x: Haah—!
Huai Tianpei leaps into the air, and sends his fists into the chest of 'Shuo'.
He cuts a ragged figure, but his eyes are crystal clear.
As the illusion dissipates...
Huai Tianpei falls from the cliff.
A wind blows by, and the sky is restored to its previous color. The battle that shook heaven and earth might as well have been a dream that everyone went through together.
The spectators continue to look up at the sky, forgetting to turn their gazes away.
Zuo Le?
Yun... Auntie Lin...
Thank goodness you're alright. I guess I wasn't too late...
Master Huai fell from the cliff in the fight with the illusion. Please send someone to search for him...
Master Huai is here too? What a coincidence...
Lieutenant!
Yes, sir.
Someone fell from the cliff. Send men to look for him.
Yes, sir.
I came with a platoon when I heard the thief who stole the Grandmaster's Book of Kung Fu from Yumen had been sighted causing trouble.
But the culprit—
Only now do they realize that the girl has disappeared without a trace.
She got away again.
All the routes in and out of the mountains are being watched. She won't escape.
As for the rest of you... ahem!
By imperial decree, those present are deemed to have been incited by an outlaw, and your culpability in this matter will not be pursued if you leave this place at once.
Those who do not comply will be dealt with harshly.

Many of those present have not always lived on the right side of the law, and their hearts sank like a stone when they saw the soldiers arrive. It is thus a massive relief to hear that they will not be prosecuted, and they bow in apology before leaving as quickly as they can.
Only the geists remain, not quite understanding the situation, looking lost and bewildered.
*pant* *pant* Made it just in time...
Look at you, acting like the worst of humanity, engaging in vanity and avarice! What did I teach you?

If you want to become human, you must learn humanity little by little, starting with right and wrong! You may have the body of a human, but if you don't have a conscience, then you're not human after all, just a beast wearing clothes!
You've seen how the book you longed for was just a dream...
Come with me back to the school, if you still want to learn...
Mr. Pu sees Lin Qingyan and seemingly bows in apology.
He turns around and leaves, in as much a hurry as he came. He is limping a little; perhaps the climb has taken a toll on his legs.
The geists linger for a little bit, then turn back into their original forms one by one, forming a long line as they follow the old man down the mountain.
Surrender now!
A crack of thunder knocks the girl's blade out of her hands and sends her to the ground.
Dark clouds hover above her head, ready to deliver an even more powerful thunderclap.
I suggest you don't move, unless you wish to be struck by lightning.
Persistent lot, aren't you, Master Lin and Candleholder Zuo?
A peaceful end, and no harm was done. Do you really need to prosecute me so?
All the people you dragged into this 'show' of yours...
It is no mean feat, summoning a Sui-Xiang with the token of a Feranmut proxy.
Who are you? Where did you learn such forbidden arts?
Is that all you care about?
The most ancient secret in this land is within reach, Candleholder, and all you want to do is to arrest everyone who tries to understand it?
Objects have masters, but knowledge has none. Who decided that the Sui Regulator, and no one else, is allowed to know?
How am I supposed to believe that you have no malicious intent, given what you did at Mangshan Town?
You'll come with us, and you'll paint a clear picture of everything you know, and everything you're planning.
So you want to take me alive. Does that mean there's nothing you can do, should I insist on running?
Obstinate fool...
We want you alive, but not necessarily conscious—
One moment!
A crack echoes in the sky, but the fire is faster.
The blade of flame cuts through the thunder, and a figure lands in front of the Liberi.
Your Lei Fa is quite impressive for your age.
Your Arts are familiar. Does Bai Dingshan's generation have their own students now?
These fire Arts...
Could you be—Zushi?!
(Isn't this the girl who played with Grain Buds in Dahuang?)
I... I am Lin Qingyan, student of the Lei Fa school, and of the Tianshi Bureau.
This isn't a formal occasion. No need for formalities.
You too, girl.
...Mo Yi of the Shanhaizhong. Greetings, Tianshi.
(So she's really Shanhaizhong.)
Why the look of surprise? Go to the scrolls in the deepest sections of your library. You are the ones who are behind in terms of Feranmut research.
All I see is the Shanhaizhong committing evil deeds of every kind in the name of the Feranmuts.
What have I to do with those who commit murder, arson, or any other deeds under the Shanhaizhong name? How is it any of my business?
Can you guarantee that everyone who wears that shiny uniform of yours is an upstanding individual?
You—
These eccentric ways, this sharp tongue of yours... you're just like your founder.
I thought the old carpenter didn't have any heirs left, after all these years. Looks like I was wrong.
The great hunt a thousand years ago contradicted his ideals. He became disillusioned and shied away from society...
But some people remember how he wanted to stop the war.
You talk big. How do you intend to stop this war?
I simply wanted to see the master of the Garden of Grotesqueries. It was for this reason that I stole the Book of Kung Fu and summoned the Sui-Xiang.
I guarantee that this war can be stopped, if I'm allowed to enter his garden to find a certain object.
...A remnant of the proxy who disappeared.
You have nerve, if nothing else...
Round-head!
You've been listening long enough. Are you going to welcome your guests or not?
All the guests present today are very important...
You certainly made my heart skip a beat with your suggestion... Or you would, if I had one...
Enough small talk. This has more to do with you than anyone else. What do you plan on doing?
I spoke with my master just now... since both you and a Candleholder of the Sui Regulator are here, may I make a proposal...
May we have our guests assist in the Placation for this year, as it seems fate has intended?
Placation?
The Sui Regulator checks on the slumbering bestial Sui and reinforces the seal on the Tomb of Sui. This process is called the Placation.
But it has always been the most experienced Candleholders who handle the affair...
The rogue proxy entered the Tomb of Sui several days ago. There has been no news since. The Sui Regulator is likely vexed about to how to conduct this year's Placation.
This is a major matter. I must report to—
You really think there's time to make a report?
Just go on. If the Grand Tutor asks, tell him I gave the nod.
U-Understood...
You, girl, you're coming with me.
You don't get along with that Candleholder kid, and you'll just keep fighting in there. Whatever you're looking for, he can look for it for you.
As for you, I want to talk to you about that master of yours.
...As you wish.
What should we do?
It's not hard. Just go up along the path once you enter the Garden of Grotesqueries, and pay attention to your surroundings.
Other than that, be careful of collections in the Garden of Grotesqueries... the geists of the master's collection are a far cry from those you saw in the town. You must be careful.
You said go up? But we're already at the top of the mountain, aren't we?
Where is this 'Garden of Grotesqueries' that you speak of?
Infinitely far, and infinitely close... when you are on this mountain, you are adjacent to the Garden of Grotesqueries.
Taking you into the Garden of Grotesqueries is easy, but reach its depths requires the touch of fate—may I borrow the weiqi board that you carry with you?
Of course... go ahead.
And may I have the tome you are carrying, young lady?
...Whatever.
Thank you...
A passage will now be opened. Go through this passage, and you may enter the forbidden grounds of the Garden of Grotesqueries.
Lines of war, warp and weft, in the order of heaven and earth—

The weiqi board and book extend infinitely into the distance from Liang's hands, creating a realm within a realm. Mountains turn to flatland in the blink of an eye, and the group goes through this strange space—
They step out of it to find a door hidden behind a screen.
Welcome to the Garden of Grotesqueries.
Here we are.
This was a town full of vitality before that disaster, with hundreds of households and flourishing farmland...
Many years have passed, and now all that is left is—
Come out.
You.
Yes, Master Liang.
Are all of you here?
(Looks left and right)
There are some broken tiles and bricks amidst the ruins. Not much time has passed since they awakened, and they do not yet understand language... should they be brought out?
Let me explain why I'm here first.
All of you must leave this place.
(Whispering to one another)
Everyone who lived here has either died or departed after that incident at the Tomb of Sui.
You are mundane artifacts that have gained sentience from the incident's aftermath, wandering this place...
Perhaps you've already sensed the presence of a garden above the tomb, which Yi calls the Garden of Grotesqueries.
Neither Yan nor the siblings will tolerate the presence of a geist-infested ruin in the vicinity of the Garden of Grotesqueries.
We understand.
But where can we go, Master Liang?
Yi said those of good form or quality can find a home in the garden. The rest must be cleaned up.
You have one month to prepare. All traces of the disaster should be gone by then.
The east area has been cleaned up.
We'll sow some grass seeds here, then clean up the west area. It used to be a river that flowed through the town, but it'll take some time to clean up the sludge.
Good work.
We've gathered the geists that meet the requirements, with reference to the book that you brought... they're all here. There aren't many.
Go and help them, then come with me to the Garden of Grotesqueries afterwards.
It's busy here.
Indeed.
Where is Yi?
He went out.
He found the surroundings of one of the pavilions a little too sparse, and went looking for something to decorate there.
It seems like you're the only one around, whenever I visit.
That's why he built me.
He can be quite communicative... to people and things that he's interested in.
He would talk three days and nights to a child just to obtain a stone, even if scolded and abused, but he can't stand to be around your kind for even a few minutes.
That's for the best, I suppose. I couldn't stand his fake hospitality either.
Back on topic. Are things not proceeding a little slow? Seven days have passed, and only a small part of the ruins has been cleared.
Slow?
Clearing the rubble is the easy part. But properly handling hundreds if not thousands of objects touched by geisthood... I'm sure the Sui Regulator knows full well how arduous a task it is.
There's an easier way, no?
If my ears didn't deceive me, some Waregeists are following you back to the Garden of Grotesqueries...
Do you mean that I should take all the geist-touched objects, bricks and tiles, weeds and branches, chairs and tables, cups and plates... back to the Garden of Grotesqueries?
Yes.
We don't have the space.
Didn't you say that Yi thought the garden was a little sparse?
Did I?
The awakening of Sui caused great upheaval in Baizao and the surrounding lands. Your siblings have paid a dear price, even...
Stop.
Yi says that is all in the past. The siblings do not wish to speak of it, especially not with you.
In any event, the affairs of the Garden of Grotesqueries have always been the affairs of the people.
It sits atop the tomb, pinning the slumbering one in a dream, pacifying the evil that would threaten Baizao...
I think you're mistaken about something.
Oh?
The Garden of Grotesqueries is first and foremost Yi's garden.

Home to the rare and delightful, the eccentric and the grotesque from all four corners, from ancient times to present, Yi would love to have the sights of the whole world in its boundaries, and would gladly move the entire garden's layout for the sake of one little rock...
It is not a tool for the Sui Regulator, and certainly not a trash heap for the Tomb of Sui.
I will deal with the geists near the tomb. The Sui Regulator should not presume to interfere with anything else.
Hasn't the east side been cleaned up? Why is this Waregeist going that way?
Someone's coming. Stay out of sight.
This is it, this is the place...
At long last...
Oh, sorry, I didn't realize there was somebody here... er, y-you're human, right?
Nothing you need to worry about.
Well, er... sir? Are you one of the mandarins assigned to watch these hills, judging by your attire?
You could say that.
This place has long been abandoned, the roads broken and overgrown. No civilian has approached it in many years. Who are you?
You might know there was once a town here. I used to live here.
It was a long time ago.
Yes.
A disaster happened many years ago, a terrible event that no one could truly describe. The town was destroyed overnight...
I was a young boy at the time. My whole family was... it was by sheer luck that I survived.
I went to Jiangqi and lived there for many years. This is the first time I've been back.
Jiangqi is a long way from here.
A very long way.
I spent most of the money that I saved over the years on this journey.
Just to see these... ruins?
No, I plan to rest for a couple of days, then get about fixing this house and clearing some farmland around here.
I think my journey has come to an end.
I don't understand. You've found a new life in Jiangqi. Why are you obsessed with these broken ruins?
They may look like ruins to you, broken beams and cracked bricks overgrown with weeds...
But after all these years, I still remember how I hid my pocket money in a corner of the house, to buy sweets from a passing peddler...
I remember fighting with the neighbor's kid, bringing lunch to our parents in the fields together, going to class at the school on the west side together...
This is my home, sir, even if it was destroyed.
As you say, it was destroyed.
You remember the disaster. Aren't you afraid it may strike again?
Catastrophe is everywhere on this land. There is no haven that has never been touched by disaster.
Disaster might interrupt our lives, but we'll come back after it's gone. We'll fix the houses, clean up the fields, again and again... as it always has been, for centuries and more.
I met others who fled this place before I set out on the journey. They want to come back, just like me. Before long...
Given enough time, even objects form an attachment to a place, never mind humans.
But you can't stay here... at least, not for a few days yet.
Huh?
There are official restrictions in place for this area right now. Go and stay at a nearby inn and wait for the announcement.
Alright.
Ouch—did I trip over something?
It's nothing. I'll see you off.
Come out.
Silence.
I suggest you come out while I'm still asking politely.
There is rustling in a corner, and a Waregeist in the shape of a bottle emerges from its hiding place after a while.
...It was you.
You were the one who was running around when the Sui Regulator came, weren't you?
This place has been cleaned up. Why are you hiding here?
Mm...
You don't want to be destroyed?
(Shakes up and down)
Do you want to come with me to the Garden of Grotesqueries?
(Shakes left and right)
You want to stay here?
(Shakes up and down)
Why?
I'm waiting for someone, sir.
Waiting for someone?
The ones who made me.
A scholar by the name of Lan Ke and a woman by the name of Bai Jin made me, when this place was still a town. They said I was the first thing they made for their new home.
When they got married, they put me in the most prominent place in the living room and put a new flower in me every day. Never forget.
They shaped me and put me in the furnace, then went off to do other things while I was being heated...
Then the disaster happened, and they never came back.
I... I've been waiting for them...
You're not going anywhere?
I'm not going anywhere.
Sir...
Please don't be angry at it, sir.
The scroll-shaped entity stands before Liang and angles its upper half forward, its bamboo strips turning white at the effort.
Are you... bowing?
Indeed.
I learned that one should bow politely when making a request.
Oh?
There used to be a school on the west side of town, and I laid on the top layer of the bookshelf.
The teacher told the students, 'Manners maketh man.' He said it many times, and I remembered.
It didn't mean to disobey your orders, but it has had no other thought in its mind for so many years.
Could you, perhaps, oblige it?
The scroll-shaped entity once again bows.
I won't let you hurt it, no matter your answer!
A sword-shaped entity puts itself in front of the bottle-shaped entity and scroll-shaped entity, pointing its head, the tip of the sword, towards Liang. Its blade reflects the sunlight.
It's my... my...
Kindred? Friend?
Yeah, that.
I have a few questions for you, bottle.
Yes, sir.
When did you start thinking that way?
...I don't remember.
Have you ever doubted?
Why should I doubt?
That man from Jiangqi came back. So will Lan Ke and Bai Jin.
Liang stands in silence, as the wind blows through his hollow skull and chest mechanism, causing a soft humming sound.
He looks like he is thinking.
I thought the contamination had caused you to form basic sentience, no more than that.
You wander around this place, your words and actions limited by your attributes as objects...
Little did I imagine that you would develop human-level thoughts and emotions...
Given enough time, even objects form an attachment to a place, never mind humans.
Interesting.
You are Waregeists that have lingered after the disaster. Do you remember what the town looked like?
We do, sir.
Can you restore it?
Yes.
Are you willing to live together with humans?
What have you been doing behind my back, Liang?
What is it?
I told you to clean up the ruins, destroy the Waregeists that should be destroyed, and bring back the Waregeists that should be brought back to the Garden of Grotesqueries...
But you built a town?
You're talking like you don't know.
I'm your creation. My thoughts are your thoughts. My deeds are your deeds.
Hah. I see, I see.
Besides, this is the best solution, isn't it?
There are too many geist-touched objects around the Tomb of Sui. Simply destroying them or throwing them into the garden won't solve the problem.
People are returning to this town, long after it was turned to ruins...
Your account of the changes in the geists is interesting.
But the Sui Regulator must have complained about letting geists live together with people. What did you say to them?
I told them we'll keep an eye.
'Yi and I, just the two of us, are going to have a hard time watching over all the geists, if all we have is a single garden.'
'What if the geists get out? It would be like what happened to Mangshan Town: The Sui Regulator would take the brunt of it, then Baizao.'
Haha, good.
I never have to worry, with you in charge.
Xiexie.
Anyway, it's a good thing.
A town in the proximity of the Garden of Grotesqueries will delay those with ill intent, giving us time to welcome our guests.
Now, clean the garden, and let us welcome our visitors.
